blockingIssues.description: Navbar collapse uses `media.xl` where `xl` is `maxWidth: 1024`, so collapsed mode remains active at 1024px. Feature contract requires collapse only for <1024px and full desktop nav at 1024px.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: When specifying responsive breakpoints in feature contracts, explicitly state inclusive/exclusive boundary semantics (e.g., '<1024' vs '<=1024') and align with framework media-query behavior.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: This re-run failed due to an off-by-one mismatch between feature contract expectation (<1024 collapsed) and Tamagui `maxWidth: 1024` semantics used in implementation.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Make the mandatory TDD step conditional for CSS/responsive/dependency-only changes and provide a visual/computed-style-first validation path for those cases.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The same mismatch was observed again in this re-run review after being raised in round 1; workers continue reporting TDD-procedure misfit for non-functional styling/config-only fixes.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add explicit agent-browser command examples for viewport changes (e.g., `set viewport <w> <h>`) to avoid trial-and-error during visual validation.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Review transcript skeleton for this fix shows repeated failed viewport commands before discovering correct syntax.
